reforms.description: Canada is promoting the application of information and communication technologies (ICT) in health care to improve quality and cost-efficiency, including the use of electronic health records (EHR). Additionally, the establishment of an independent, pan-Canadian agency to monitor health-care quality and provide advice on best practices is proposed to enhance accountability and improve health outcomes. A comprehensive review of all federal support for research and development is also announced to improve its contribution to innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The integration of ICT in health care is expected to enhance efficiency and quality, leading to better health outcomes and potentially reducing costs.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms are proposed and have not yet been implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms involve significant changes in health care delivery and monitoring, but do not constitute a major overhaul of the system.
reforms.source_quote: Creating an independent, pan-Canadian agency to explicitly monitor health-care quality and provide advice on clinical and institutional best practice could importantly support this work.

reforms.description: The federal government has invested over CAD10 billion since 2006 to reduce greenhouse gas emissions and promote sustainable environmental practices through investments in green infrastructure, energy efficiency, and clean-energy technologies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investments in green technologies are expected to foster innovation in sustainable practices and contribute to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The investment has been ongoing since 2006.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant investment in R&D for environmental sustainability, marking a major policy shift.
reforms.source_quote: Since 2006, the Government of Canada has invested more than CAD10 billion to reduce greenhouse gas emissions and build a more sustainable environment through investments in green infrastructure, energy efficiency and clean-energy technologies.

reforms.description: Ontario proposed to complete the phase-out of its tax credit for Labour-Sponsored Investment Funds by 2012.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The phase-out of this tax credit may reduce incentives for investment in certain funds, potentially hindering innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The proposal has been made but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is a minor adjustment to existing tax policy rather than a major overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: Ontario proposed to complete the phase-out of its tax credit for Labour-Sponsored Investment Funds by 2012.
